title: 3.3V Logic Rail (Low Noise)
description: A low-ripple 3.3 V rail derived from a 5 V bus, targeting microcontroller and digital logic applications where voltage noise degrades ADC accuracy and RF performance.
design_notes: The 600 kHz switching frequency and 20 % ripple ratio work together to produce a small 6.8 µH inductor and a compact 22–47 µF output capacitor, allowing a very small PCB footprint. The 20 mV ripple target (0.6 % of 3.3 V) is tight enough for most microcontrollers and 12-bit ADCs without requiring additional LC post-filtering. If the design powers an RF transceiver or precision DAC, add a second LC stage (e.g. 1 µH + 10 µF) after the switcher to reach sub-millivolt noise. Ferrite-bead filtering on power entry pins is still recommended for sensitive analogue blocks regardless of output ripple.
source: Erickson & Maksimovic §2.4; TI SLVA677 — Practical Feedback Loop Design Considerations
